skill.name: ship-cli
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents through Ship CLI task, workspace, stack, and pull request workflows.
skill.summary: Use Ship CLI to manage tasks, stacked changes, workspaces, and pull requests.
skill.author: EduSantosBrito

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed file is Markdown workflow guidance rather than executable code. The cryptography alerts are false positives, while the Ship CLI workflow can perform repository and task side effects such as rebasing, pushing, creating PRs, and marking tasks done. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External Workflow Actions With Repository And Task Side Effects
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use Ship CLI actions that can sync stacks, create workspaces, push changes, create pull requests, and mark tasks complete. This matches the skill purpose, but it can change repositories and task systems, so users should confirm intent before publication use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited workflow entries explicitly describe stack submission, pushing, PR creation, and task completion. The behavior is legitimate for a Ship CLI workflow, but the side effects are clear.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified As Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command detections come from Markdown inline code, code fences, and command names used as documentation. No Ruby shell backtick execution or executable script code was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only reviewed source file is Markdown and the flagged syntax appears in documentation examples. There is no executable Ruby code or shell backtick evaluation in the file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Plain Markdown Text
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings point to frontmatter, tables, and prose. No evidence found of cryptographic API use, hashing logic, credential handling, or security-sensitive implementation code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown metadata, parameter names, or table rows, not cryptographic operations. The skill contains no executable code where a weak algorithm could be invoked.

content.value_statement: Development work can drift across tasks, workspaces, and pull requests. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Ship CLI task and stack workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to start work from ready tasks and mark Linear tasks in progress.
Defines a Ship CLI stack workflow for creating, describing, syncing, and submitting changes.
Documents workspace handling with consistent workdir usage for bash and Ship CLI actions.
Provides task description structure with summary, acceptance criteria, and notes.
Explains PR review, stacked PR, milestone, and troubleshooting actions available through Ship CLI.
limitations: Requires the Ship CLI tool to be available in the agent environment.
Does not implement Ship CLI commands or provide executable scripts.
Assumes Linear and GitHub workflows match the documented Ship CLI actions.
Can affect repositories and tasks when the connected tool executes requested actions.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Assigned Engineering Work
use_cases.description: Start a ready task, create a workspace, and keep the task state aligned with implementation progress.

use_cases.title: Submit Stacked Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Create, describe, sync, and submit stacked changes while preserving clear PR and task context.

use_cases.title: Review Ship CLI Workflow Options
use_cases.description: Find the right Ship CLI action for tasks, milestones, pull requests, or stack troubleshooting.

prompt_templates.title: Start A Ready Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a new assigned item with Ship CLI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ship-cli skill to find ready work, start the selected task, create a workspace, and tell me the workspace path.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare A Stacked Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a clean change with a useful commit description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ship-cli workflow to create a stack change for this task and set a clear title and description before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Submit Completed Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Finish implementation and open a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Follow the ship-cli submit workflow. Sync the stack, submit the change, verify that the PR exists, then mark the task done.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Stack And PR Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Resolve review feedback across a stacked workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ship-cli reference to inspect unresolved PR feedback, update the current stack, restack if needed, and report the final status.

output_examples.input: Start my next Ship CLI task.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies ready tasks, asks which one to start if needed, marks it in progress, and reports the workspace path.
The agent syncs the stack, submits the current change, confirms the pull request exists, and only then marks the task complete.

output_examples.input: What should I do after a PR webhook says merged?
output_examples.output: The agent explains the event, asks for confirmation before acting, and suggests syncing the stack or checking ready work.

best_practices: Confirm the selected task and workspace before running actions with side effects.
Use explicit workdir values for every command after a workspace is created.
Mark tasks done only after the pull request exists and the submission result is verified.
anti_patterns: Running git, jj, gh, or ship directly through bash when the Ship tool is available.
Acting automatically on webhook suggestions without user confirmation.
Using vague task descriptions without acceptance criteria or verification steps.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It guides AI agents through Ship CLI task, workspace, stack, and pull request workflows.

faq.question: Does it run commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is Markdown guidance, but connected agents may execute Ship CLI actions when users request them.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can it create pull requests?
faq.answer: It documents Ship CLI actions that can create or update pull requests through the connected tool.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but its workflow can modify repositories, pull requests, and task status.

faq.question: Does it contain cryptography code?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The static cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown text.
